message.content:
# minimatch — Security Knowledge Base Entry

## 1. Package Risk Summary

minimatch is a widely depended-upon glob-matching library with a history of Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS) vulnerabilities in its pattern-processing logic, requiring repeated hardening across all supported major release lines.

## 2. Known Vulnerabilities

| Severity | CVE | GHSA | Description | Fixed Versions | Sources |
|----------|-----|------|-------------|----------------|---------|
| High | CVE-2016-10540 | GHSA-hxm2-r34f-qmc5 | ReDoS via crafted glob pattern | ≥ 3.0.2 | [GitHub Advisory](https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-hxm2-r34f-qmc5) |
| High | CVE-2022-3517 | GHSA-f8q6-p94x-37v3 | ReDoS through long or specially crafted pattern strings | ≥ 3.0.5 | [GitHub Advisory](https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-f8q6-p94x-37v3) |
| — | CVE-2026-26996 | GHSA-3ppc-4f35-3m26 | ReDoS in glob/extglob/GLOBSTAR pattern processing | 3.1.3, 4.2.4, 5.1.7, 6.2.1, 7.4.7, 8.0.5, 9.0.6, 10.2.1 | [GitHub Advisory](https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-3ppc-4f35-3m26) |
| — | CVE-2026-27903 | GHSA-7r86-cg39-jmmj | ReDoS in glob/extglob pattern processing | 3.1.3, 4.2.5, 5.1.8, 6.2.2, 7.4.8, 8.0.6, 9.0.7, 10.2.3 | [GitHub Advisory](https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-7r86-cg39-jmmj) |
| — | CVE-2026-27904 | GHSA-23c5-xmqv-rm74 | ReDoS in glob/extglob pattern processing | 3.1.4, 4.2.5, 5.1.8, 6.2.2, 7.4.8, 8.0.6, 9.0.7, 10.2.3 | [GitHub Advisory](https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-23c5-xmqv-rm74) |

## 3. Security Posture Notes

- **Extremely high adoption**: minimatch registers over 107 million weekly npm downloads, making any vulnerability high-impact across the ecosystem.
- **Recurring vulnerability class**: All five known CVEs are ReDoS issues, indicating that regex-based pattern matching has been a persistent and systemic attack surface in this package.
- **Broad major-line exposure**: The three most recent advisories (CVE-2026-26996, CVE-2026-27903, CVE-2026-27904) required coordinated patch releases across eight major version lines (3.x through 10.x), reflecting wide version fragmentation in the consumer base.
- **Incremental hardening approach**: The upstream changelog documents the addition of "basic redos protection" in the 3.x line, suggesting mitigations have been applied progressively rather than through a single comprehensive rewrite.
- **Vulnerability clustering in specific subsystems**: Public advisory details indicate that glob, extglob, and GLOBSTAR matching logic are the primary areas where backtracking vulnerabilities have been discovered.
- **Upstream responsiveness**: The maintainer has published security advisories and fix commits for all known issues, with fixes backported to all actively maintained major lines.
- **Single-maintainer project**: The repository (isaacs/minimatch) is attributed to a single primary author, which is common for foundational npm packages but introduces bus-factor risk for security response.

## 4. Recommendations for Developers

- **Pin to the latest patched release within your major line**: Ensure you are running at least the fixed version for your major line (e.g., ≥ 3.1.4, ≥ 4.2.5, ≥ 5.1.8, ≥ 10.2.3, etc.) to incorporate all known ReDoS mitigations.
- **Never pass untrusted or user-supplied strings as glob patterns**: All known vulnerabilities are triggered by crafted pattern input, so treat the pattern argument as a trusted-input-only API surface wherever possible.
- **Monitor for future ReDoS advisories**: Given the recurring nature of backtracking issues in this package, subscribe to GitHub security advisories for `isaacs/minimatch` and enable automated dependency vulnerability scanning (e.g., `npm audit`, Dependabot
